heading: Association of marine insurance companies; application of antitrust laws
label: 38 Association of marine insurance companies; application of antitrust laws
notes: CodificationSection was classified to section 885 of the former Appendix to Title 46, prior to the completion of the enactment of Title 46, Shipping, by , , .Pub. L. 109–304120 Stat. 1485Oct. 6, 2006
amendments: CodificationSection was classified to section 885 of the former Appendix to Title 46, prior to the completion of the enactment of Title 46, Shipping, by , , .Pub. L. 109–304120 Stat. 1485Oct. 6, 2006

children.text: The term “association” means any association, exchange, pool, combination, or other arrangement for concerted action; and

The term “marine insurance companies” means any persons, companies, or associations, authorized to write marine insurance or reinsurance under the laws of the United States or of a State, Territory, District, or possession thereof.

children.label: b
children.text: Nothing contained in the “antitrust laws” as designated in , shall be construed as declaring illegal an association entered into by marine insurance companies for the following purposes: To transact a marine insurance and reinsurance business in the United States and in foreign countries and to reinsure or otherwise apportion among its membership the risks undertaken by such association or any of the component members.section 12 of this title
